title: NEGOTIATING A PURCHASE
conversation: Max is shopping for a used car. He goes to see Jim who's selling his used Honda. After a test drive, Max negotiates with Jim and gets him to reduce the price by $800. Jim: Hello. Jim Harris speaking. Max: Hello, Jim. My name is Max Taylor. I'm shopping for a used car, and I saw your ad for a 2008 Honda Civic. Jim: The car is still available, but I'm getting lots of calls for it. Max: How about if I swing by at 5:30? Jim: Okay. I'm on 433 Main Avenue, across the street from Arroyo High School. (Max comes over to Jim's house.) Max: Hi, I'm Max. I'm here to see the car. Jim: Good to meet you, Max. I'm Jim. Would you like to take a test drive? Max: Yes, thanks. The last thing I need is another lemon! Jim: You don't have to worry about that. This is a great car. (They return from the drive.) Max: It does drive well. Jim: And it's in mint condition! Max: You're asking $8000 for it? Jim: Yes, it's a steal. Max: It's a nice car, but it's a two-door car and I was hoping to buy a four-door. Jim: A car like this is going to sell quickly. I'd hate for you to miss out. Max: I'll give you $7,000. Jim: Let's split the difference. If you can make it $7,500, you've got a deal. Max: I'd like to sleep on it. Can I give you a ring in the morning? Jim: I'd like to reach an agreement now. How about $7,300? Max: Would you take $7,200? Jim: I don't want to haggle. Max: I'm sorry to nickel and dime you, but money is tight for me right now. Jim: Okay, let's close the deal. I'll take $7,200.

idioms.meaning_en: to visit a place briefly on your way to somewhere else
idioms.example: I'll swing by your office this afternoon to pick up the documents.
idioms.context: How about if I swing by at 5:30?

idioms.meaning_en: to drive a motor vehicle to test its condition before buying it
idioms.example: Always take a test drive before you sign any car purchase papers.
idioms.context: Would you like to take a test drive?

idioms.meaning_en: something that you do not want at all
idioms.meaning_vi: điều cuối cùng tôi muốn (điều tôi thực sự không muốn)
idioms.example: The last thing I need right now is more work.
idioms.context: The last thing I need is another lemon!

idioms.meaning_en: a vehicle that turns out to have many defects or doesn't work properly
idioms.meaning_vi: chiếc xe bị lỗi, xe rác (không đáng tiền)
idioms.example: I realized the car was a lemon when the engine failed after just one week.
idioms.context: The last thing I need is another lemon!

idioms.meaning_en: in perfect condition, as if new
idioms.meaning_vi: trong tình trạng hoàn hảo, như mới
idioms.example: He keeps his old comic book collection in mint condition.

idioms.meaning_en: a bargain; something bought at a very low price
idioms.example: At only twenty dollars, this designer jacket is a steal.

idioms.meaning_en: to fail to use an opportunity to enjoy or get something
idioms.example: Don't miss out on the early-bird discount; book your tickets now.
idioms.context: I'd hate for you to miss out.

idioms.meaning_en: to agree on an amount halfway between two proposed amounts
idioms.meaning_vi: chia đôi phần chênh lệch (lấy mức giá ở giữa)
idioms.example: You want $100 and I want $80, so let's split the difference and say $90.

idioms.meaning_en: I accept your offer or proposal
idioms.meaning_vi: chốt đơn, đồng ý thỏa thuận
idioms.example: If you can finish the project by Friday, you've got a deal.
idioms.context: If you can make it $7,500, you've got a deal.

idioms.meaning_en: to wait until the next day before making a decision
idioms.example: I'm not sure about the job offer yet; I need to sleep on it.
idioms.context: I'd like to sleep on it.

idioms.meaning_en: to call someone on the telephone
idioms.example: I'll give you a ring when I arrive at the airport.
idioms.context: Can I give you a ring in the morning?

idioms.meaning_en: to arrive at a mutual decision or settle a deal
idioms.example: After hours of negotiation, both sides finally reached an agreement.
idioms.context: I'd like to reach an agreement now.

idioms.meaning_en: to argue or bargain over the price of something
idioms.example: In many street markets, it's expected that customers will haggle.

idioms.meaning_en: to argue or bother about small amounts of money
idioms.meaning_vi: kèo nèo, chi li từng đồng
idioms.example: The airline will nickel and dime you for every little thing, from bags to water.
idioms.context: I'm sorry to nickel and dime you, but money is tight for me right now.

idioms.meaning_en: having very little money; experiencing financial difficulty
idioms.meaning_vi: tiền bạc eo hẹp, đang kẹt tiền
idioms.example: Money is tight this month, so we can't afford to go out for dinner.
idioms.context: I'm sorry to nickel and dime you, but money is tight for me right now.

idioms.meaning_en: to finalize a business transaction or agreement
idioms.meaning_vi: chốt giao dịch, hoàn tất thỏa thuận
idioms.example: The salesman was happy to finally close the deal and sign the contract.
